commit.message:
Verify & correct natural experiments census report (EXP-1551-1558)

Round 4: 461-line report, 417 claims verified accurate, 11 corrections:

- Patient-days total 1,858→1,838 (arithmetic error in table sum)
- Median experiments/patient-day 26.5→27.0
- Correction rate ratio 185×→~309× (0.1→0.06/day for patient k before rounding)
- Per-patient-day rate 27.4→27.6
- Overall mean quality 77%→76%
- Archetype clusters corrected per EXP-1556: g→High-freq AID, h→Moderate/Manual,
  k→Correction-heavy (3 patients reassigned)
- Fasting ≥1/day: removed patient b (0.74/day), noted f marginally at 0.98
- Meal template peak 45→65 min (actual template median peak index)
- Fasting drift −0.5→−3.5 mg/dL/h (template shows steeper decline)
- Detector yields: correction 4.2→4.0/day, AID response 5.1→4.8/day

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: publish-on-change from C++ setters, eliminate polling

Replace three 3s polling lambdas + 500ms interval with direct
publish from C++ setters:
- InternetRadio: publishes now_playing and station_select from
  loop() (title change) and set/next/prev_station_()
- WinampDisplay: publishes brightness from set_brightness()
- cycle_brightness() now delegates to set_brightness() (DRY)
- Remove WiFi AP (useless without captive_portal/web_server)
- All HA entities use update_interval: never

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix dashboard interview start entry point

Ensure job seekers always see a working start interview CTA on dashboard, including first visit and no-open-job fallback.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: autostop triggers from morning radio automation instead of media_title (#29)

The media_title condition didn't match (likely 'Evropa 2 - Radio' vs
'Evropa 2'), so the autostop never fired.

Simplified approach: trigger when the morning radio automation's
last_triggered attribute changes, then delay 2 hours and stop both
players. No conditions needed since the parent automation already
handles time/weekday checks.

Mode changed to 'restart' so re-triggering the radio resets the
2-hour timer.

Refs: #25

Co-authored-by: Petr Vavro <petrvavro@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add FEATURE-058-F acceptance test report (94% pass rate)

- Frontend-UI: 12 pass, 1 partial, 1 blocked (test env)
- Backend-API: 4 pass
- Unit: 2 pass

Minor issue: AI Agent button not visually disabled when no graphs
selected (shows error message instead). Terminal pre-fill blocked
in test environment (no active pty sessions).

commit.message:
chore: remove Loki stack — replaced by Dozzle

Loki was 15 pods / 870MB RAM. Replaced with Dozzle (1 pod / 20MB)
for real-time log viewing. Freed ~32Gi of Longhorn PVCs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Verify & correct alert-filtering, confidence-intervals, temporal-models reports

Round 3: 3 reports verified against experiment JSON data.

alert-filtering-report.md (EXP-1611-1618) — 12 corrections:
- EXP-1612 state hypo rates: low-risk 15-42%→45-79%, postprandial 5-12%→1-10%,
  correction 6-11%→0-8%, fasting 3-8%→2-11%, fasting count 4→5/11
- EXP-1613 AUC 0.90→0.89, feature importance reordered (std_1h is #3, not rate)
- EXP-1615 reduction 84-87%→83-87%
- EXP-1616 alert range 25-33→11-33
- EXP-1617 burst rate 91%→90%, actionability 19%→21%
- Clarified 93% burst as max (mean=90%) throughout

confidence-intervals-report.md (EXP-1621-1628) — 4 corrections:
- Added missing patient j row to EXP-1621 table (ISF=95, CI=[41,115], 78%, n=7)
- ISF median CI width 46%→47% (including all 10 patients)
- CR/ISF ratio 10×→~9×
- Conformal coverage: 7/9 meet 90% target (not 9/9; c=88%, i=82%)

temporal-models-report.md (EXP-1631-1638) — 5 corrections:
- EXP-1632 12h harmonic gain +0.195→+0.289, 8h gain +0.178→+0.122
- EXP-1635 time period definitions corrected to match source code
- Most variable period count: afternoon 4→3 patients
- Parameter counts: sinusoidal 2→3, harmonic 8→9 (intercept was omitted)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Decouple SinglePassMode from FastMode for independent control

- SinglePassMode now controls multi-turn vs single-call independently
- FastMode only affects model choice (cheaper model) and brevity constraint
- Added single-pass variant to PM agent spec creation (was 2 turns)
- Researcher (3 turns) and Architect (5 turns) already had single-pass
- Added SinglePassMode to appsettings.template.json

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Q1 2026 record output: 100 adopted texts including Banking Union,
  anti-corruption directive, and US tariff countermeasures
- Post-Easter outlook: committee week April 14-17, Strasbourg plenary April 20-23

  threat assessment, and coalition dynamics

commit.message:
[go][js][py][sh] update (error) copy (#449)

* [ai][go] Rewrite user-facing error messages in cmd/radar for voice quality

- Replace 'Failed to X' with calmer 'Could not X' phrasing
- Remove 'Error:' prefixes from log.Printf messages
- Switch units/timezone messages from '%s' to %q for quoted output
- Add diagnostic hints (--help suggestions, valid values, next steps)
- Add example usage to --log-level validation message
- Keep all format verbs, control flow, and function signatures unchanged

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: ddol <445312+ddol@users.noreply.github.com>

* [ai][go] Improve migrate CLI error messages with diagnostic hints

- Add usage examples to invalid version number errors for force/baseline
- Replace 'Cannot read/determine' with calmer 'Unable to' phrasing
- Add hint about database access for version read failures
- Add hint about embedded migration files for latest version failures

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: ddol <445312+ddol@users.noreply.github.com>

* [ai][go] Improve user-facing HTTP error messages for voice quality

Rewrite error strings in writeJSONError() and http.Error() calls across
seven source files for clearer, calmer British English:

- Replace 'failed to X' with 'could not X'
- Replace 'missing X parameter' with 'the X parameter is required'
- Replace 'no background manager for sensor' with diagnostic hint
- Replace 'invalid JSON' with 'the request body is not valid JSON'
- Replace 'method not allowed' with 'this endpoint only accepts METHOD requests'
- Replace 'database not configured' with hint about --db-path
- Add diagnostic hints to terse messages
- Lowercase 'Could not' to 'could not' for consistency

Update corresponding test assertions to match new messages.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: ddol <445312+ddol@users.noreply.github.com>

* [ai][web] Improve user-facing error messages for voice quality

Rewrite mechanical error messages across the web frontend:

- api.ts: Replace 'Could not X: STATUS' with 'Could not X — the server
  returned status STATUS' for ~50 throw-site messages. Add articles
  where appropriate for natural phrasing. Rename 'radar stats' to
  'radar statistics' and 'parameters' to 'tuning parameters'.
- settings: Fix broken includes('Failed') checks — error messages
  have always used 'Could not', never 'Failed'.
- tracks: Replace 'Failed to load' console message with 'Could not'.
- MapEditorInteractive: Rewrite Overpass API error with context and
  add missing full stop to bounding box validation message.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: ddol <445312+ddol@users.noreply.github.com>

* [ai][python] Soften user-facing error messages across Python tools

Replace harsh 'Failed to X', 'Error: X', and '✗ NOT FOUND' patterns with
calmer phrasing ('Could not X', direct descriptions). Add diagnostic hints
where missing. British English throughout.

Files changed:
- tools/pdf-generator/pdf_generator/core/map_utils.py
- tools/pdf-generator/pdf_generator/cli/main.py
- data/align/get_telraam.py
- data/align/get_unifi.py
- tools/grid-heatmap/plot_grid_heatmap.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: ddol <445312+ddol@users.noreply.github.com>

* [ai][sh][docs] rewrite shell script messages and mark voice audit plan complete

Co-authored-by: ddol <445312+ddol@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Export tier CSV after ordinal bucketing completes

Automatically sends a tier_assignments.csv with all judges and their
tier (1-5, S, C) when bucketing finishes, before moving to the
per-tier refinement prompt.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Ordinal: only bucket judges without existing ratings

Judges that already have scores (from Notion match or CSV pre-fill)
are kept as-is and skip the bucketing phase. Only unrated judges are
presented for tier assignment.

commit.message:
Fix HAP SRP pairing: downgrade to srp 0.5 + working pair_setup.rs

Replace dependency-updates branch pair_setup.rs with the working main
branch version, using srp 0.5 APIs (SrpClient, UserRecord, get_key).
Downgrade sha2 0.10→0.9, hkdf 0.12→0.11, chacha20poly1305 0.9→0.8 to
match srp 0.5's digest 0.9 requirement. Add rand 0.7. Keep libmdns 0.7
to preserve the ifaddrs linker fix.

Resolves 'setup code is incorrect' errors. Full M1→M6 handshake now
completes and iOS successfully pairs with the bridge.

commit.message:
fix: bearer token creation truncates client_name and returns wrong expiry for existing tokens (#22167)

* Initial plan

* fix: bearer token creation returns wrong expiry and truncates client_name

Two bugs in src/web/api/http_auth.c:

1. bearer_create_token_internal: strncpyz used sizeof(bt->cloud_account_id)
   (16 bytes) instead of sizeof(bt->client_name) (64 bytes) when storing
   the client name, silently truncating it to 15 characters. This broke the
   dedup logic in bearer_create_token (strncmp would never match names
   longer than 15 chars) and saved incorrect data to on-disk token files.

2. bearer_create_token: when an existing matching token was found in the
   in-memory dictionary, the function returned the local `expires_s`
   variable (initialized to 0) instead of bt->expires_s (the actual token
   expiration). This caused bearer_get_token_json_response to return
   "expiration": 0 (Unix epoch) to the dashboard, making the dashboard
   believe the bearer token had already expired even though it was valid.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/netdata/netdata/sessions/c468bb15-629f-46e3-ae1d-dd9694dc656d

commit.message:
fix(build): prevent 0-byte static pages from I/O contention

- Reduce parallel write batch from 300 to 200 to lower I/O pressure
- Add sync repair pass after flush: detects and rewrites 0-byte files
- Log individual write failures with file paths (was only logging count)
- flatContentPlugin: skip copying 0-byte source files to flat paths

Root cause: writeFile uses O_TRUNC which creates 0-byte files before
writing content. Under heavy I/O contention (153K+ parallel writes),
some writes fail after truncation, leaving 0-byte files that Google
indexes as soft-404s.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use Python tzdata package instead of OS tzdata for IANA timezone lookups (#2)

The apt tzdata package provides system zoneinfo files but not all POSIX
timezone keys (like CST6CDT). Python's tzdata package provides complete
timezone data that zoneinfo.ZoneInfo() needs for device-reported timezones.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: kasa-pinger

commit.message:
fix: move FFT to Core 1 to eliminate audio jitter

Core 0 callback now only accumulates mono samples into a double buffer
(~microseconds). FFT computation runs on Core 1 in draw_frame_() where
the ~100μs cost is negligible within the 55-78ms frame time.

- Double-buffered sample capture: Core 0 writes, flips index when full
- spectrum_compute() called from draw_frame_ before reading bands
- Switched to magnitude² (skip sqrtf), adjusted log10 scaling
- Zero FFT overhead on audio core — eliminates streaming jitter

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add save/resume progress and ordinal ranking mode

- Save progress: downloadable .xlsx file from any scoring view (direct
  rating, pairwise compare, review). Resume by uploading the file when
  starting a new session.

- Ordinal ranking mode: new scoring option for ordinal prefs.
  Bulk strike/conflict → one-tap tier bucketing (1-5) → optional
  per-tier pairwise refinement → export ranked .xlsx (skips quotas).

- Progress file preserves full session state including Elo ratings,
  comparison history, pair queue, ordinal rankings, and tier assignments.

- Added openpyxl dependency for Excel read/write.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix version: 0.12.0 → 0.11.0 (npm version ran ahead)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(salary): replace estimates with USTAT 2024 official Ticino data

Replace rough salary estimates with official data from USTAT Ticino
(Rilevazione svizzera della struttura dei salari, cubi_RSS_02, 2024).

Key changes:
- TICINO_SECTOR_MEDIANS: all 16 sectors updated with USTAT p50 data
  by NOGA 2008 sector × position level (junior/mid/senior)
- Ticino discount corrected: was ×0.90 (10% below national), actual
  is ~0.82 (18% below) per USTAT 2024 (CHF 5,393 vs ~6,600 national)
- Min/max range widened: ×0.80/×1.25 (was ×0.85/×1.15) based on
  actual p25/p75 interquartile ratios from USTAT data
- salaryData.ts: all 15 sectors × 4 professions recalibrated to
  match USTAT Ticino figures (generally lower than previous estimates)
- Added NOGA code references per sector for traceability
- Source attribution: USTAT cubi_RSS_02 2024, BFS LSE, Salarium

Notable corrections (sectors most affected):
- Education: was overstated by 20-36% (Ticino education pays less)
- Engineering: junior/mid were 10-18% too high
- Hospitality: senior was overstated by ~29%
- Finance senior: was understated by ~44% (USTAT shows CHF 184k)
- Retail: was understated by 18-37% across all levels

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add custom Semgrep rule recognizing LogSanitizer.sanitize() as CRLF sanitizer (#1462)


* feat: add custom Semgrep rule recognizing LogSanitizer.sanitize() as CRLF sanitizer

Create .semgrep/crlf-injection-logs-carlos.yml with a taint-mode rule that
declares LogSanitizer.sanitize() and Encode.forJava() as valid pattern
sanitizers for CRLF injection in logs. This resolves ~128 false positive
alerts across 24 files.

Also adds .semgrep/README.md documenting the custom rules, which built-in
rules to disable in Semgrep Cloud, and maintenance instructions.

Fixes #1431

Co-authored-by: yingbull <8680161+yingbull@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Michael Yingbull <michael@maplecreekmedical.ca>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Enforce foundation-first pattern in PE engineering plan

PE Agent now generates engineering plans with a mandatory T1 foundation
task that scaffolds the project structure, shared interfaces, base
classes, and DI registration before any parallel work begins.

Changes to PE planning prompt:
- T1 must always be Project Foundation & Scaffolding (High, no deps)
- All other tasks must depend on T1 for parallel safety
- Tasks must own distinct files (vertical slices, not horizontal layers)
- Star-topology dependencies: maximize tasks that depend only on T1
- FilePlan must list exact file ownership per task

Post-parse enforcement (EnsureFoundationFirstPattern):
- Reorders foundation task to first position if AI misplaced it
- Clears dependencies from T1, adds T1 dependency to all others
- Detects and logs file ownership overlaps between tasks

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: real-time 16-band FFT spectrum visualizer

Port of src/spectrum.cpp to ESPHome with zero new dependencies:
- Self-contained 128-point radix-2 FFT (no esp_dsp linkage)
- audio_process_raw_samples weak override on Core 0
- Hann window + 16 logarithmic frequency bands
- Winamp gradient bars with fast attack (0.6) / slow decay (0.15)
- spectrum_ready guard prevents use before init
- C++ linkage matches Audio.h weak declaration (not extern C)
- ~3KB RAM overhead, ~2-4% Core 0 CPU (same as original firmware)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
